justification: This paper centers on causal inference for nowcasting/forecasting GDP and recessions using textual data, requiring rigorous evaluation of identification strategies and model specifications (Econometrician), sophisticated NLP data processing and feature engineering from Beige Book text (Data_Scientist), and assessment of real-world applicability for Federal Reserve policy and regional economic analysis (Policymaker).

This paper centers on causal inference for nowcasting/forecasting GDP and recessions using NLP-derived sentiment measures, requiring rigorous econometric evaluation; involves extensive text processing and feature engineering from Beige Book data; and directly addresses Federal Reserve policy applications with real-world forecasting implications.

This paper centers on causal nowcasting/forecasting using NLP-derived sentiment measures with regression specifications requiring rigorous identification strategy assessment (Econometrician), relies heavily on text processing, feature engineering, and ML model implementation like FinBERT (Data_Scientist), and directly addresses Federal Reserve policy applications including recession prediction and regional economic monitoring (Policymaker).
